message:
fix(arch): replace broken ASCII cell diagram with CSS grid, widen algorithm cards

Two visual issues on the /architecture page:

Cell diagram (16-byte cell model):
- The ASCII box-drawing diagram (cellDiagram from lib/content.ts) rendered
  at text-[11px] font-mono looked broken—misaligned columns, ugly
  overflow-x-auto horizontal scrollbar on smaller viewports, and
  box-drawing characters that don't render consistently across browsers at
  that size.
- Replaced with a proper CSS grid layout: a header row showing "Cell
  (16 bytes)" and a 5-column grid with proportional widths
  (4fr_4fr_4fr_2fr_2fr matching actual byte sizes). Each field shows
  name, byte size, and type detail. Responsive text sizes, hover feedback,
  and clean green-on-black aesthetic matching the rest of the page.
- Removed unused cellDiagram import.

Algorithm cards (Monster Math section):
- At xl:grid-cols-4, cards were too narrow (~280px) for their content
  (long titles like "Bayesian Diff Strategy", descriptions, formulas,
  category badges, impact ratings).
- Worse: FrankenGlitch on card titles rendered 3 visible copies of each
  title (the original at z-10 plus 2 split-slice overlays at 50% opacity
  with x-offsets). On narrow cards, this produced obviously duplicated
  readable text rather than a subtle distortion effect.
- Removed FrankenGlitch wrapper from AlgorithmCard titles—now a clean
  motion.h3 with color-on-hover instead of glitch effect.
- Reduced grid from xl:grid-cols-4 to max lg:grid-cols-3, giving each
  card ~33% more width for comfortable reading.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
